sections.title: What are dependent prepositions? (1)
sections.content:
**Prepositions** are small words that show relationships (time, place, direction, topic, etc.): at 5 o'clock, in the room, on the table, to the station, about the project.

Some prepositions are **tied to a particular word** – usually a verb, noun, or adjective. These fixed combinations are called **dependent prepositions**.

sections.title: What are dependent prepositions? (2)
sections.content:
Examples:
• I'm **interested in** history. (not *interested on*)
• It **depends on** the weather. (not *depends from*)
• She's **good at** maths. (not *good in maths*)
• We complained **about** the noise.

Here the preposition is part of the pattern. Changing it usually sounds wrong or changes the meaning.

sections.title: Why they matter (1)
sections.content: Dependent prepositions are very common in exams and real life. They are often tested in **gap-fill** and **open cloze** tasks where the missing word is a preposition.

sections.title: Why they matter (2)
sections.content:
There is no single rule that works for all words – you need to **learn each pair or phrase as a chunk** (e.g. depend on, interested in, solution to).

Reading, listening, and noticing patterns helps. So does focused practice and using a **reference list** when you forget.

sections.title: Verbs, nouns, and adjectives with prepositions (1)
sections.content:
We talk about three main groups:

• **Verbs + preposition:** depend on, listen to, wait for, apply for, complain about, insist on, believe in, suffer from
• **Nouns + preposition:** reason for, solution to, answer to, increase in, relationship with, interest in, fear of, demand for

sections.title: Verbs, nouns, and adjectives with prepositions (2)
sections.content: • **Adjectives + preposition:** interested in, good at, afraid of, proud of, tired of, responsible for, worried about, keen on, capable of

sections.title: Verbs, nouns, and adjectives with prepositions (2)
sections.content:
Often the **noun pattern mirrors the verb pattern**: interested in → interest in; complain about → complaint about.

A full reference list is available from **Reference → Dependent prepositions: Verbs / Nouns / Adjectives**.

sections.title: How this topic is organised (1)
sections.content:
In this topic you will:

• **Learn and test**: basic ideas and common patterns with verbs, nouns, and adjectives (+ quick test).
• Do **guided practice**: easier gap fill with high-frequency combinations (depend on, interested in, afraid of, etc.).
• Do **further practice**: more varied gap fill with trickier or more formal combinations.

sections.title: How this topic is organised (2)
sections.content: Use the **Dependent prepositions** reference sheets while you work – they are designed to go with this topic.

questions.question: Which preposition completes the phrase? 'It depends ____ the weather.'
Choose the correct noun + preposition pair.
Which preposition completes the phrase? 'She's afraid ____ spiders.'
Which option completes the sentence? 'They complained ____ the noise.'
She's good ____ maths.
questions.explanation: Dependent preposition (adjective): good **at** a subject or skill.

questions.question: We're looking ____ a new flat.
questions.explanation: Dependent preposition (verb): look **for** something (search for it).

questions.question: She applied ____ the job.
questions.explanation: Dependent preposition (verb): apply **for** a job or course.

questions.question: We're waiting ____ the bus.
questions.explanation: Dependent preposition (verb): wait **for** someone or something.

questions.question: He's proud ____ his team.
questions.explanation: Dependent preposition (adjective): proud **of** someone or something.

questions.question: I'm tired ____ working late every night.
questions.explanation: Dependent preposition (adjective): tired **of** something (you've had enough).

gapfill_common.title: Further practice: Verbs, nouns, and adjectives + preposition

questions.question: We need a solution ____ this problem.
She has a good relationship ____ her boss.
He has a lot of experience ____ teaching.
questions.explanation: Dependent preposition (noun): experience **in** a field (experience in teaching).

questions.question: We congratulated her ____ winning the prize.
He suffers ____ severe headaches.
questions.explanation: Dependent preposition (verb): suffer **from** an illness or problem.
